doc.title: Prior Service Cost — Plan Amendment
doc.event: On January 1, Delgado Co. amends its pension plan to grant employees credit for prior years of service. The actuary determines the amendment increases the projected benefit obligation by $180,000. Prepare the entry to record the amendment.

entries.label: Amendment day — PBO up $180,000, nothing hits income yet

lines.why: The full cost of retroactive benefits parks in OCI on the amendment date.
lines.trap: Not a debit to Pension Expense. It reaches expense only through amortization in future periods.

lines.why: The obligation grew by 180,000 the moment the benefits were granted.

memorize.body: PSC hits OCI at the grant date, then trickles into pension expense via amortization. It NEVER goes straight to expense.

questions.prompt: On the amendment date, by how much does pension EXPENSE increase?

distractors.feedback: Nothing hits expense on day one — the 180,000 goes to OCI and reaches expense only through amortization.
Amortization starts in the periods FOLLOWING the grant — on the amendment date itself, expense impact is zero.

build.scaffold: Two lines. Ask: did any cash move? Did any expense belong to THIS period yet?
